title: Head of Revenue Operations
description: Finout, a cloud cost management SaaS trusted by The New York Times, Wiz, Elastic and Lyft, seeks a Head of Revenue Operations to build and scale the operational backbone of its GTM engine. Reporting to the CRO, this hands-on leadership role requires 7+ years in Revenue Operations, Sales Ops or Strategy within high-growth B2B SaaS, experience scaling beyond $30M+ ARR, deep Salesforce administration and revenue analytics expertise, and cross-functional leadership across Sales, Marketing, Finance and HR, collaborating with the Tel Aviv HQ.
score_reason: Omri's 15+ years in B2B SaaS RevOps/GTM, Salesforce expertise, and pipeline forecasting background align perfectly with Finout's Head of RevOps requirements. The hybrid New York/Tel Aviv structure matches his location preferences and the fintech/cloud infrastructure space suits his strong fit profile.
ai_opener: Omri Gonen here—I've spent 15+ years building RevOps engines for high-growth B2B SaaS companies, and Finout's approach to cloud cost intelligence resonates deeply. Your hybrid model bridging New York and Tel Aviv is exactly the setup I thrive in, and having scaled GTM operations through multiple ARR inflection points, I'm intrigued by the opportunity to build the operational backbone for a platform trusted by companies like Wiz and Lyft.

title: RevOps Manager
description: HopSkipDrive, a technology marketplace solving complex transportation challenges, is hiring a RevOps Manager reporting to the Director of Revenue Operations. The role requires 3-5 years in Sales Operations or RevOps within a high-growth B2B SaaS or marketplace environment, strong analytical skills, Salesforce proficiency, and experience with tools like Tableau and Coefficient. Responsibilities include CRM hygiene, sales enablement, pipeline forecasting, pricing updates, renewal workflows and incentive compensation administration. Salary range is $90k-$120k per year.
score_reason: Strong technical fit with Salesforce, pipeline forecasting, and B2B SaaS marketplace experience, but role is significantly junior (3-5 years required vs. 15+ years experience) and salary is well below market for a senior RevOps executive. Omri would likely be overqualified and may experience role dissatisfaction.
ai_opener: I came across HopSkipDrive's RevOps Manager opening and was impressed by your marketplace approach to transportation—complex unit economics and multi-stakeholder management are exactly where RevOps creates outsized impact. While the role targets 3-5 years of experience, my 15+ years building RevOps functions from 0-to-scale in B2B SaaS (including marketplace dynamics) might bring a different value perspective if you're open to considering someone who could architect your entire revenue infrastructure rather than execute within it.

title: RevOps Manager, Territory Strategy & Operations
description: Gong, the revenue intelligence platform, is hiring a RevOps Manager for Territory Strategy & Operations. The role requires deep understanding of B2B SaaS go-to-market models, sales territories and segmentation strategies, strong analytical skills with Excel, SQL and BI tools (Tableau, Retool), and Salesforce proficiency. Responsibilities include redesigning territory models for global scalability, partnering with Sales leadership and Finance to optimize territory compositions, and maintaining accurate territory mapping. Salary range is $149,000-$175,000 USD.
score_reason: Exceptional match for Omri's RevOps expertise and GTM background. Territory strategy, Salesforce proficiency, pipeline optimization, and B2B SaaS scale-up experience align perfectly with core responsibilities.
ai_opener: Omri, Gong's RevOps Manager role for Territory Strategy & Operations caught my attention given your 15+ years building GTM infrastructure in B2B SaaS. Your pipeline forecasting and territory optimization experience would directly address their global scalability challenges—especially redesigning territory models and partnering with Sales leadership on segmentation strategies that Gong is actively building.

title: GTM Automation Engineer
description: Postscript, a 100% remote company backed by Greylock and Y Combinator, seeks a GTM Automation Engineer to build AI-driven systems at the intersection of GTM, Data and AI tools. Requires 4+ years in Revenue Operations, Sales Engineering or Business Systems, understanding of the B2B SaaS customer lifecycle, proven automation workflow expertise, comfort reading API docs, writing SQL and debugging Python with AI tools like Claude Code, Cursor and Replit. You'll build agents that research prospects and automate customer touchpoints.
score_reason: Exceptional alignment with Omri's RevOps expertise, B2B SaaS depth, and AI-native GTM focus. The role's emphasis on automation workflows, API integration, and Python/SQL debugging matches his technical RevOps skillset perfectly, though it skews slightly more engineering-heavy than his typical IC-to-leadership trajectory.
ai_opener: Omri here—your GTM Automation Engineer role caught my attention because it's exactly where RevOps is heading: AI agents that actually understand the sales lifecycle rather than just executing playbooks. With 15+ years building B2B SaaS GTM systems and hands-on experience automating complex workflows across Salesforce and HubSpot, I've spent the last few years exploring how Claude and similar tools can turn revenue operations from reactive to predictive. I'd love to explore how to build the kind of prospect research and touchpoint automation systems you're describing.

title: GTM Engineer
description: Pulumi, a remote-first infrastructure-as-code SaaS company, seeks a GTM Engineer for its Growth team. This high-ownership builder role involves developing AI-powered tools, workflows and automations to accelerate sales and marketing teams. Projects include automating paid digital programs, improving visibility in AI search platforms and accelerating trial conversion. Ideal candidates have a developer tools or technical B2B SaaS background, can translate GTM problems into systems solutions, and experience with AI-native workflows. Base salary range is $100,000-$140,000.
score_reason: Excellent match on GTM Engineer role, AI-native workflows, and B2B SaaS infrastructure focus. Remote Pacific Time works globally; salary slightly below typical senior RevOps market but role offers high ownership and technical depth that aligns with Omri's builder mentality.
ai_opener: Omri here—your GTM Engineer role caught my attention because it's exactly the intersection I've been optimizing for: translating RevOps complexity into AI-powered systems that actually move pipeline. I've spent 15 years building automation frameworks in B2B SaaS (HubSpot/Salesforce), and developer tool GTM has always been the most intellectually satisfying—partly because the customers actually understand what you're building. Would love to explore how my background in paid digital automation and AI-native workflows could accelerate your sales/marketing velocity.

title: GTM Engineer (Remote)
description: Unframe is hiring a GTM Engineer to build the systems that power its GTM motion and enterprise sales execution. You'll design and implement automation, tooling and data infrastructure that turns GTM strategy into repeatable pipeline, working directly with Sales Leadership, Enablement, Marketing and Solutions. Responsibilities include building scalable outbound infrastructure (account selection, sequencing, enrichment, routing), designing automations and integrations across Salesforce and the GTM stack, and creating GTM Agents that support seller productivity and execution.
score_reason: Exceptional match - GTM Engineer role aligns perfectly with RevOps expertise, requires Salesforce/stack integration design, pipeline automation, and data infrastructure that are core to Omri's background. Remote location and enterprise sales focus on B2B SaaS GTM motion are ideal fit.
ai_opener: Hi [Hiring Manager], I saw Unframe is building GTM Agents and scalable outbound infrastructure - this resonates deeply with my 15+ years architecting RevOps systems that turn strategy into predictable pipeline. I've spent the last decade designing exactly this kind of automation layer across Salesforce, enrichment tools, and sales execution stacks for B2B SaaS teams scaling from $5M to $100M+ ARR. I'd love to explore how my experience building account selection logic, sequencing automation, and seller productivity tools could accelerate Unframe's GTM motion.

title: Lead GTM Engineer (Remote)
description: Engine, a fast-growing business travel platform serving 20,000+ companies, is building a Growth Engineering function and seeks a Lead GTM Engineer to own and shape it. This founding role focuses on creating systems, signals and automations that help GTM teams reach the right companies at the right time. Requires experience building production software in growth/marketing/sales-adjacent environments, strong Python proficiency, experience integrating GTM systems like Salesforce and Outreach, and prior people leadership experience or readiness to lead a team.
score_reason: Strong alignment on GTM strategy, systems thinking, and Salesforce/Outreach integration expertise. Primary gap is production software engineering requirement—Omri's strength is RevOps strategy and tooling orchestration rather than building software from scratch, though GTM engineering is closer to his domain than pure engineering roles.
ai_opener: Hi—I noticed Engine is building out a Growth Engineering function from the ground up. With 15+ years scaling B2B SaaS GTM motions and deep expertise in Salesforce/Outreach system design and pipeline automation, I'm interested in how you're thinking about the signals and systems layer. I've built most of the operational infrastructure that growth engineers typically depend on—would be curious to explore whether that adjacent experience could help shape this founding role.

title: GTM Engineer
description: Redwood Software, maker of the first SaaS-based composable automation platform for ERP, seeks a GTM Engineer to lead design, implementation and optimization of AI-driven tools and automations across the marketing department. The role ensures the marketing organization leverages AI safely and efficiently, from campaign automation to content production and analytics. Requires 5+ years in marketing operations, marketing technology or demand gen with understanding of B2B GTM, plus API-driven integration skills across the MarTech stack.
score_reason: Strong alignment with GTM Engineer title, B2B SaaS, and AI-native focus. However, role is heavily marketing operations/MarTech-focused rather than broader revenue operations, and requires marketing tech integration skills that may not be Omri's primary expertise as a senior RevOps executive.
ai_opener: Omri, Redwood's GTM Engineer role caught my attention given your RevOps background and track record optimizing B2B SaaS go-to-market engines. With your expertise in pipeline forecasting and cross-functional GTM strategy, you'd bring rare perspective to architecting AI-driven automation across marketing operations—especially for an ERP automation platform where process efficiency directly mirrors your core strength.

title: Head of Growth
description: Workerbase, a Series A software platform digitizing manual work processes (trusted by Porsche, Siemens and Gazelle Bikes, backed by €10m from Point 9, Almaz Capital and Porsche Ventures), seeks a Head of Growth to own and lead growth strategy spanning acquisition, activation, retention and revenue. You'll drive measurable impact across inbound funnels and outbound experiments, define and optimize the entire funnel from first touch to customer expansion, and set the standards for how the company scales.
score_reason: Strong alignment with Head of Growth mandate and RevOps/GTM expertise in a Series A B2B SaaS company with solid backing. However, the role emphasizes broad growth leadership (acquisition/activation/retention) rather than deep RevOps infrastructure, which is Omri's core strength—better fit would be a dedicated RevOps or GTM Operations role as the company scales.
ai_opener: I noticed Workerbase is scaling post-Series A with marquee enterprise customers like Porsche and Siemens—that's impressive traction. With 15+ years building GTM infrastructure and revenue operations at B2B SaaS scale, I've spent a lot of time designing the funnel architecture and forecasting rigor that compounds growth. I'd be curious whether you're looking to layer systematic RevOps into your growth engine as you scale.

title: Head of Growth
description: Sagetap is hiring a Head of Growth to lead its growth function. This role focuses on building and scaling demand generation, acquisition and revenue growth strategies for a fast-growing B2B technology company. The position requires experience owning the full growth funnel, running acquisition experiments, and partnering cross-functionally with sales and marketing teams to drive measurable pipeline and revenue impact in a fast-paced startup environment.
score_reason: Strong alignment with Head of Growth mandate covering demand generation, pipeline strategy, and cross-functional revenue leadership. Remote location is ideal; primary concern is company stage/scale maturity given 'startup' descriptor needs validation.
ai_opener: Omri here—15+ years scaling B2B SaaS revenue from pipeline to close. I've built demand generation playbooks and GTM strategies that drive predictable pipeline growth, and I'm impressed by Sagetap's focus on owning the full growth funnel. I'd love to explore how my experience with acquisition experiments and sales/marketing alignment could accelerate your revenue trajectory.

title: Growth Marketing Campaigns Manager
description: Vanta, a trust management SaaS platform with offices in SF, NYC, London, Dublin, Tel Aviv and Sydney, seeks a Growth Marketing Campaigns Manager to own integrated GTM campaigns end-to-end. Requires 5+ years in B2B SaaS demand generation or growth marketing, experience owning integrated programs (email, paid, lifecycle, events) from brief to results, strong campaign data analysis skills, working relationships with Sales/SDR/creative/Product Marketing, and proficiency in HubSpot and Salesforce.
score_reason: Strong technical fit with HubSpot/Salesforce expertise and B2B SaaS background, but role is growth marketing campaigns (demand gen focused) rather than RevOps/GTM leadership where Omri's 15+ years would be best leveraged. This is a solid but slightly lateral move from his target seniority level.
ai_opener: Omri here—I've spent 15+ years building GTM engines in B2B SaaS, and I've watched Vanta scale into one of the most respected compliance platforms. Your integrated campaign approach resonates with how I think about pipeline orchestration, and I'd be curious whether you're also looking to strengthen the RevOps/GTM operations layer that powers campaigns like these.

title: Marketing Operations Manager, Campaign Ops
description: Bloomerang seeks a Marketing Operations Manager for Campaign Operations, reporting to the Director of Marketing Operations. You'll execute marketing campaigns end-to-end in Marketo or HubSpot, manage data workflows and provide reporting for a fast-growing B2B SaaS marketing organization. Requires 5-7 years of Marketing Operations experience in B2B SaaS, hands-on expertise with HubSpot or Marketo, and familiarity with the broader revenue tech stack including Salesforce, Outreach, LeanData and Chili Piper. Fully remote within the US and select Canadian provinces.
score_reason: Strong technical alignment with HubSpot/Marketo expertise and B2B SaaS background, but this is a specialized marketing ops IC role reporting to a director—below Omri's seniority level as a senior RevOps/GTM executive. Better suited for a Head of Marketing Operations or Revenue Operations leadership position.
ai_opener: Hi [Hiring Manager], I noticed Bloomerang is building out Campaign Ops under Marketing Operations—I've spent 15+ years architecting GTM infrastructure across B2B SaaS, including deep Salesforce, HubSpot, and revenue tech stack integration. While this role is more hands-on execution than my typical scope, I'm curious if you're planning to expand this function into a broader RevOps/GTM leadership mandate—that's where I typically create the most impact.

title: GTM Engineer
description: Supabase, a fully remote developer platform company, seeks a GTM Engineer to solve real-world problems for developers and deploy applied AI in a sales context. Requires 3+ years in a GTM Engineering, Sales Engineering or RevOps Engineering role at a B2B SaaS company, comfort working with HubSpot APIs and custom objects, SQL/BigQuery, and building automations with Python or Node.js. Experience in async or globally distributed teams is valued. Benefits include fully remote work, ESOP, tech allowance and health benefits.
score_reason: Strong alignment with GTM Engineer role, B2B SaaS background, HubSpot expertise, and remote work preference. The AI-native GTM focus and developer platform context match Omri's profile well, though the role emphasizes technical automation (Python/Node.js/SQL) which may be secondary to his RevOps leadership experience.
ai_opener: Omri here—your GTM Engineer role caught my attention because it bridges something I've built my career around: applying systematic, data-driven approaches to revenue operations at scale. Having spent 15+ years optimizing B2B SaaS GTM motions and HubSpot deployments, I'm genuinely excited about the intersection you're exploring—embedding applied AI directly into sales workflows for developer platforms. I'd love to explore how my experience building RevOps systems could accelerate how Supabase moves developers from activation to expansion.

title: GTM Engineer
description: WorkOS, a developer-focused platform helping SaaS applications become enterprise-ready, seeks a GTM Engineer to own the systems that identify, enrich, score and engage prospects at scale. You'll maintain core enrichment and fit-scoring pipelines, build systems that sync event attendee lists into Salesforce campaigns, work with data engineering on the Snowflake schema, and ship real code with PR reviews. Requires 3+ years in GTM Engineering, Revenue Operations or a closely adjacent technical role at a high-growth B2B SaaS company.
score_reason: Excellent match for a senior RevOps executive—GTM Engineer role aligns perfectly with pipeline forecasting, data systems, and B2B SaaS expertise. The technical depth (Snowflake, Salesforce syncs, prospect enrichment) leverages 15+ years of RevOps infrastructure building, though this is slightly more IC/technical than typical Head of RevOps trajectory.
ai_opener: WorkOS's approach to building enterprise-ready infrastructure resonates with my philosophy on RevOps—that GTM systems should be as robust as product infrastructure. With 15+ years scaling revenue operations in B2B SaaS, I've built the exact enrichment, scoring, and Salesforce automation pipelines you're describing, and I'd bring both strategic vision and hands-on execution to own this end-to-end.
